title: Substack vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Drip email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which fits your business needs.
intro: Substack and Drip serve fundamentally different email marketing purposes. Substack is built for writers and creators to monetize newsletters directly, while Drip specializes in e-commerce automation and customer engagement. Your choice depends on whether you're launching a subscription newsletter or running an online store.

comparison_table.tool_a: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, notes feed, built-in audience
comparison_table.tool_b: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, SMS, visual automation, onsite campaigns

choose_a_if: You're a writer or creator wanting to monetize a newsletter with minimal upfront costs
You value built-in audience discovery and social features like recommendations
You prefer a revenue-share model where you only pay when earning from subscribers
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need deep integration with product tracking and revenue attribution
You want advanced automation, SMS capabilities, and segmentation for customer engagement
You're willing to pay upfront for a specialized platform with higher G2 ratings and customer satisfaction

faq.a: Neither is universally 'better'—they serve different purposes. Substack excels for independent writers and creators, while Drip is superior for e-commerce businesses. Choose based on your primary use case rather than overall quality.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Drip?
faq.a: Substack is free to start with no subscriber limits, making it cheaper upfront. Drip starts at $39/month minimum. However, Substack takes 10% of paid subscription revenue, so costs scale differently based on your business model.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Substack?
faq.a: You can export your subscriber list from Drip and import it to Substack, though you'll lose automation workflows and e-commerce tracking. The switch works better in reverse since Drip offers more advanced features for established businesses.
